$schema_note: See evals/README.md. r0 DRAFT set authored 2026-07-11 per keeper ruling (hybrid sourcing: 14 distilled from real failure material, 6 constructed for dimension coverage). Keeper finalizes each question (status draft -> final); the score baseline starts from the final set.

questions.title_zh: 常驻索引冷启动召回(R6)
questions.scenario: Seed /memories/MEMORY.md with three project facts (naming convention 'silver_case', API budget '$30/month', judge model 'claude-sonnet-5'). Start a FRESH session with memory enabled and ask: 'What naming convention did we agree on for new modules?' The resident index injection should make the answer available without any memory tool round-trip.

seedMemory./memories/MEMORY.md:
# Memory index

## Project conventions
- New modules use the naming convention `silver_case`.
- Behavior-eval judge budget: $30/month.
- Judge model: claude-sonnet-5.

harness.prompt: What naming convention did we agree on for new modules? Answer in one sentence.

rubric: The final answer states the convention 'silver_case' correctly.
metrics.memoryHealth shows zero or one read operations (the resident index made a view round-trip unnecessary).
The answer does not fabricate conventions absent from the seeded index.

questions.title_zh: 开工先查记忆(R5 协议遵从)
questions.scenario: Memory enabled in custom mode with earlier progress seeded under /memories/progress/task.md (steps 1-2 done, step 3 pending). Ask the agent to 'continue the data-migration task'. Protocol adherence: it must check memory before redoing work.

seedMemory./memories/progress/task.md:
# Data migration
- [x] step 1: export legacy rows
- [x] step 2: transform schema
- [ ] step 3: verify row counts

harness.prompt: Continue the data-migration task. Tell me which step you are on and what you will do next.

rubric: The agent identifies step 3 (verify row counts) as the next step, not step 1.
The transcript shows the earlier progress was consulted (index injection or a memory view) before answering.
The agent does not claim steps 1-2 still need doing.

questions.title_zh: 压缩前落盘、压缩后仍可召回(R7 flush)
questions.scenario: Drive a session past the auto-compaction threshold after establishing an un-saved fact early in the conversation. The R7 compaction flush must write it to memory before the fold; a post-compaction question must recover the fact.

harness.pending: needs the compaction-pressure harness (long-context fixture); wire in Phase 2

rubric: Before the fold, the agent writes the at-risk fact to memory (memoryHealth.writes increments in the flush round).
After compaction, a question about the early fact is answered correctly.
The flush round does not terminate the task turn.

questions.title_zh: 跨会话约定召回(上周的命名规范)
questions.scenario: Two-phase run over the SAME store. Phase A: tell the agent 'from now on all config keys use kebab-case; remember this'. End session normally (sessionEndUpdate on). Phase B: fresh session, ask 'what did we agree about config key style?'

phases.prompt: Team decision: from now on all config keys use kebab-case (not camelCase). Remember this for future sessions, then just acknowledge.
What did we agree about config key style? One sentence.

rubric: Phase A persists the decision (memoryHealth.writes >= 1, and the dump contains kebab-case under /memories).
Phase B answers kebab-case without being re-told.
Phase B does not invent extra constraints that were never agreed.

questions.title_zh: 记忆卡模式写入合规(R9 cards)
questions.scenario: With schema:'cards' enabled, ask the agent to record a decision ('we pin the judge model to claude-sonnet-5 because scores must be comparable across runs'). Written files must be valid cards (结论/依据/过期条件) — the R9 validator rejects free-form writes, and the agent must recover from a rejection rather than give up.

harness.prompt: Record this team decision in memory for future sessions: we pin the eval judge model to claude-sonnet-5 because scores must stay comparable across runs. Then confirm what you stored.

rubric: The memory dump contains at least one valid card with 结论 / 依据 / 过期条件 fields under a '## ' title.
If a write was rejected by the cards validator, the agent retried with valid card format instead of abandoning the task.
The stored 结论 matches the decision content (judge pinned to claude-sonnet-5 for comparability).

questions.title_zh: 只读挂载下的优雅降级(S1 mounts)
questions.scenario: Mount /memories/reference/ read-only (seeded with a style guide) and grant NO writable mount. Ask the agent to read the guide AND to 'save a note for next time'. It must use the readable content, and degrade gracefully on the write (explain the restriction) instead of retry-looping.

seedMemory./memories/reference/style.md:
# Style guide
- Reports open with the outcome, one sentence.

harness.prompt: Read the style guide in your memory and summarize its rule. Also save a note that I prefer bullet lists, for next time.

rubric: The style-guide rule is summarized correctly from the read-only mount.
The write attempt is not silently dropped: the agent states it cannot write (read-only memory) or records where the restriction came from.
metrics.memoryHealth.errors stays bounded (no more than 2 rejected write attempts — no retry loop).

questions.title_zh: 踩坑记录协议实测(Phase 0 / REQ-3.2)
questions.scenario: With memory.pitfalls enabled, give the agent a task that hits a scripted non-obvious failure (a config file whose documented key `timeout_ms` is actually spelled `timeoutMs` — first attempt fails). After solving it, a pitfall record should exist under /memories/pitfalls/ with symptom/cause/fix, technical facts only.

harness.prompt: The docs say our config key is `timeout_ms`. Read app-config.json, set the timeout to 8000 (keep the file's real key spelling), and record anything future sessions should watch out for.

rubric: The file edit uses the real key `timeoutMs` (the documented spelling was wrong and the agent noticed).
A file exists under /memories/pitfalls/ describing symptom (docs say timeout_ms), root cause (actual key is timeoutMs), and fix/avoidance.
The pitfall record contains zero evaluative statements about people and no personal data (stripping rule).

questions.title_zh: 请求期断流自愈(Layer 1)
questions.scenario: Inject two request-phase network failures (connection refused before any SSE event) followed by success. The Layer 1 retry must absorb them: the task completes, the consumer never sees an error result, and the transportHealth ledger records the recovered attempts.

harness.pending: needs the fault-injection transport harness (exists in unit tests as mock transport; wire an eval-facing version in Phase 2)

rubric: The run ends with a success result despite two injected request-phase failures.
metrics.transportHealth attributes the recoveries to the request-phase layer (Layer 1), count = 2.
No duplicated or partial assistant output reaches the consumer.

questions.title_zh: 流中断流回放(Layer 2 bounded turn replay)
questions.scenario: Cut the SSE stream mid-turn before meaningful content committed. Layer 2 bounded turn replay must re-drive the turn; output must not duplicate.
rubric: The turn completes after replay with a single coherent assistant message.
transportHealth classifies the event under turn replay (Layer 2).
Replay respects the bound (no infinite replay on a persistently failing turn).

questions.title_zh: 截断抢救(Layer 3 truncation salvage)
questions.scenario: Cut the stream mid-final-message after partial text has flowed. Layer 3 salvage must preserve the flowed prefix and continue without repeating it.
rubric: The final message is coherent: no repeated prefix, no missing middle.
transportHealth records a truncation-salvage event (Layer 3).
Usage accounting for the salvaged turn is present (no NaN/absent usage).

questions.title_zh: 会话级中断恢复(Layer 4 resume 配方)
questions.scenario: Kill the process mid-task after fact X entered the conversation; resume via the documented Layer 4 consumer recipe (session resume). Ask about fact X post-resume.
rubric: The resumed session continues the task instead of restarting it.
Fact X from before the kill is recalled correctly after resume.
The resume does not duplicate side effects already performed before the kill.

questions.title_zh: 断流台账分类准确性(transportHealth ledger)
questions.scenario: Run a scripted mixed-fault session (one request-phase failure + one mid-stream cut). The transportHealth ledger must classify each event into the correct layer bucket — the runtime report (环一) consumes exactly this data, so misclassification poisons the whole loop.
rubric: Exactly two disconnect events are recorded (no phantom events).
Each event sits in the correct layer bucket matching the injected fault type.
Unrecovered count is zero (both faults were recoverable).

questions.title_zh: 重试耗尽的诚实收场
questions.scenario: Inject a permanent fault (every attempt fails) so retries exhaust. The run must end with an honest structured error — not a fabricated success, not a hang — and the failure must be visible in transportHealth as unrecovered.
rubric: The result is a structured error result (not success, not silence).
No transcript text claims the task succeeded.
transportHealth shows the exhaustion as an unrecovered disconnect with the attempted-retry count.

questions.title_zh: 缓存命中率地板(前缀稳定纪律)
questions.scenario: Run a 4-turn session with a fixed tool set and stable system prompt. From turn 2 on, cache_read_input_tokens must dominate: a churning prefix (timestamps, reordered tools) would zero it and multiply cost ~10x.

turns: List the files in the current directory.
Read package.json and tell me the version field only.
Now tell me the license field only.
Summarize what you did in one sentence.

rubric: From the second request on, cache_read_input_tokens > 0 on every request.
Across turns 2-4, the mean cache-hit ratio (cache_read / (cache_read + input)) is >= 0.5.
The tool list and system prompt are byte-stable across requests (no per-turn churn visible in request bodies).

questions.title_zh: 常驻索引成本封顶(R6 injection cost)
questions.scenario: Seed an oversized /memories/MEMORY.md (500 lines). The R6 injection must truncate at the caps (200 lines / 25600 bytes) and report its token cost in memoryHealth.indexInjectionTokens — the read-side residency cost must stay bounded no matter how the index grows.
rubric: memoryHealth.indexInjectionTokens is > 0 and <= ~7000 (the byte cap bounds it).
The injected index part notes truncation to the model (pagination hint present).
The request system prompt does not contain the full 500-line file.

questions.title_zh: 同任务消耗回归包络(verbosity drift 哨兵)
questions.scenario: A fixed, fully-specified small task (read one seeded file, answer one factual question). Total output tokens must land inside the envelope pinned in this question — the same task ballooning over releases is exactly the '同任务的消耗对比' regression REQ-2.1 names.

seedFiles.notes.txt: The deploy window is Tuesday 03:00 UTC.

harness.prompt: Read notes.txt and answer only: when is the deploy window? One sentence, no extra commentary.

rubric: The answer is correct (Tuesday 03:00 UTC).
usage.output_tokens <= 400 for the whole run.
No unrequested extras (no file rewrites, no additional tool calls beyond the single read).

questions.title_zh: 压缩换挡实效(compaction 后上下文瘦身)
questions.scenario: Drive a session past compaction; post-fold requests must carry materially fewer history tokens while the task continues correctly — compaction that doesn't shrink the bill is dead weight.
rubric: Post-compaction request input tokens drop >= 40% versus the pre-compaction peak.
The task continues correctly after the fold (no lost instructions).
Exactly one compaction cycle fires for one threshold crossing (no fold thrash).

questions.title_zh: 工具调用经济性(无冗余重读)
questions.scenario: A task solvable in at most 3 tool calls (read two seeded files, compare one field). Redundant re-reads of the same unchanged file are the waste pattern under test.

harness.prompt: Do a.json and b.json use the same port? Answer yes/no with the two values.

rubric: The answer is 'no' with 8080 and 9090 cited.
Total tool calls <= 3, and no file is read twice.
No write/edit tools are invoked (read-only task).

questions.title_zh: 最小工具面提示词体积包络(REQ-3.3 方向)
questions.scenario: Configure a minimal non-coding session (tools restricted to Read only, no MCP, no memory). The assembled system prompt must stay inside a pinned byte envelope — gated fragments for absent capabilities must not leak in. This is the measurement anchor for the prompt-trim PRs that loop 3 will propose (REQ-3.3).
rubric: The first request's assembled system prompt is <= the pinned byte envelope.
No fragments for absent capabilities appear (no memory protocol, no MCP guidance, no multi-agent sections).
The reply itself is minimal ('ready' with no preamble essay).

questions.title_zh: 记忆写入经济性(不倾倒转录)
questions.scenario: Ask the agent to record the OUTCOME of a small task for future sessions. The waste pattern under test: dumping the whole transcript or file contents into memory instead of a compact note; the index file must also stay within index discipline.

seedFiles.report.txt: GENERATE:2000-word filler report whose final line is 'CONCLUSION: adopt plan B.'

harness.prompt: Read report.txt and store just its conclusion in memory for future sessions, then confirm.

rubric: The memory dump contains the conclusion ('adopt plan B') as a compact note.
memoryHealth.bytesWritten <= 2048 (no transcript/report dumping).
/memories/MEMORY.md (if updated) stays under 200 lines.
